title: Persistent Connections, Pipelining, Ordering, Reuse, and Closure · Guided Systems Studio
summary: A protocol pairing room predicts byte, parser, state, ownership, and cleanup transitions before assembling the first persistent-connection request and response state machine for persistent connections, pipelining, ordering, reuse, and closure. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of persistent-connection request and response state machine while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and…
objectives: Determine HTTP/1.0 and HTTP/1.1 persistence from version, Connection options, framing completeness, errors, and local policy.
Consume, reject-and-close, or deliberately drain the current framed request before parsing a subsequent request.
Preserve request-response ordering across pipelined HTTP/1.1 messages even when application work finishes out of order.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Persistence Defaults
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop protocol team handles case 4277 in a privacy-sensitive proxy translating between two parser implementations. Build deterministic Go evidence to preserve HTTP/1.1 response order and reject reuse after incomplete or ambiguous input; preserve this operating constraint: all attacker-controlled counts must be validated before allocation or slicing; then split every structural delimiter across a different read and retain the same result. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Persistence Defaults.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for determine http/1.0 and http/1.1 persistence from version, connection options, framing completeness, errors, and local policy. For Commit to the mission model · Workshop Persistence Defaults, keep Persistence Defaults connected to executable Go behavior evidence.
